ORDER GRANTING, IN PART, AND DENYING, IN PART, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: COURTNEY M. COATES FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: DANIEL J. NEELEY MICHELLE L. LANDRY FOR DEFENDANT AND OPPOSING PARTY: ELIZABETH CHANEY HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS SET #2 IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST #27 SUBJECT TO THE ADDITIONAL LIMITATION THAT THE DOCUMENTS MUST RELATE TO CONTRACTS, OR WERE THE SUBJECT OF PROPOSALS OR OTHER NEGOTIATIONS, INITIATED BEFORE THE MARCH 8, 2004 NOTICE OF DISSOLUTION. SAID DOCUMENTS INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO, RECORDS RELATING TO HEWLETT-PACKARD AND A.P. SOLVE IDENTIFIED IN THE MARCH 22, 2004 LETTER FROM DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF AND RECORDS RELATING TO VIDUS LIMITED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING CATEGORIES OF REQUESTS IN DISPUTE. BRUCE E. CHAN.
